judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the agent grouped and summed the 'total_revenue' values as strings (not converted to numeric) and then sorted lexicographically, causing it to choose an entity with "999.88" over larger numeric totals like "2226.42", leading to the wrong top song.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python, the agent grouped and summed revenue by track_id while explicitly assuming each track_id is a distinct song (without performing the required entity resolution across duplicate track records), leading to an incorrect top song selection.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the contributing execute_python step, the agent’s entity resolution sets the entity_key to (None, None, None) whenever title_norm is None (including literal "None" titles), collapsing many unrelated tracks into one group and inflating revenue, which led it to report “None” by Anathema as the top song instead of the true track.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the entity-resolution normalization stripped all non-ASCII characters (using [^a-z0-9]+), causing many non-Latin titles/artists to collapse to an empty block_key and be merged into one entity, inflating revenue and yielding an arbitrary representative ("Свобода" by "Джан ку") instead of the true top song.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python, the agent aggregated revenue by an exact lowercased "title | artist | album" canonical key, which failed to resolve near-duplicate tracks with minor variations in the tracks table, so it did not combine all sales for the true top song and incorrectly returned "Groovey."
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@
